title: Ensembles and Cocktails: Robust Finetuning for Natural Language Generation

structured.summary: This paper introduces StyleTunedLM, a parameter-efficient fine-tuning approach using LoRA adapters to customize large language models to individual author styles while preserving instruction-following capabilities. It demonstrates improved style alignment and generation quality over few-shot and instruction-prompting baselines across multiple well-known authors, with techniques to control content memorization and merge style and instruction adapters.

strengths.point: Effective style customization with parameter-efficient fine-tuning
strengths.evidence: StyleTunedLM reduces perplexity by 7% and achieves ~87.9% style classifier accuracy, outperforming few-shot and instruction-prompting baselines across 10 authors.

strengths.point: Novel merging of LoRA adapters to combine style and instruction-following abilities
strengths.evidence: The paper proposes and demonstrates merging style-following and instruction-following LoRA modules without degrading performance, enabling a single model to both follow instructions and generate in a customized style.

weaknesses.point: Lack of comparison to recent PEFT methods for style adaptation
weaknesses.evidence: The paper omits discussion and empirical benchmarking against recent parameter-efficient fine-tuning approaches like prefix-tuning or adapter fusion techniques designed for style or persona adaptation.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient evaluation details and reproducibility information
weaknesses.evidence: Critical hyperparameters for LoRA fine-tuning, dataset splitting criteria, and style embedding extraction methods are underexplained or missing, hindering replication and interpretation.

questions.question: How does StyleTunedLM perform on truly low-resource or contemporary authors not present in the LLM pretraining corpus?
questions.motivation: Evaluating on such authors is crucial to assess generalizability and practical applicability beyond well-known literary figures.

questions.question: What is the impact of varying LoRA adapter rank and size on style capture quality and instruction-following preservation?
questions.motivation: Understanding these trade-offs would guide efficient adapter design and resource allocation.

critique_points.point_001: Lack of comparison to recent PEFT methods for style adaptation. The paper omits discussion and empirical benchmarking against recent parameter-efficient fine-tuning approaches like prefix-tuning or adapter fusion techniques designed for style or persona adaptation
critique_points.point_002: Insufficient evaluation details and reproducibility information. Critical hyperparameters for LoRA fine-tuning, dataset splitting criteria, and style embedding extraction methods are underexplained or missing, hindering replication and interpretation
